annotations.label: Stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: a stereographic projection is a perspective projection of the sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `stereographic` as an open partial homeomorphism from the unit sphere in any real inner product space (minus a unit vector v) to the orthogonal complement of v, generalizing the classical 2-sphere construction.

annotations.label: Conformality and circle-preservation

anchor.snippet: It maps circles on the sphere to circles or lines on the plane, and is conformal

annotations.note: Neither conformality of `stereographic` nor its circle-to-circle/line behavior is proved in Mathlib; only smoothness is established.

annotations.label: Not isometric or equiareal

anchor.snippet: It is neither isometric (distance preserving) nor equiareal (area preserving)

annotations.note: No statement about isometry/area-preservation failure of stereographic projection appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Induced metric from inverse projection

anchor.snippet: The metric induced by the inverse stereographic projection from the plane to the sphere defines a geodesic distance

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the pullback metric/geodesic distance obtained from inverse stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Unit sphere and north pole setup

anchor.snippet: The unit sphere

annotations.note: The unit sphere `sphere (0 : E) 1` is standard, and a unit vector `v` with `‖v‖ = 1` plays the role of the north pole in `stereographic`.

annotations.label: Stereographic projection of a point

anchor.snippet: there is a unique line through

annotations.note: `stereographic_apply` gives the closed form `(2/(1−⟪v,x⟫)) • orthogonalProjection x`, the abstract analogue of the line-through-north-pole construction.

annotations.label: Cartesian formulas for projection

anchor.snippet: In Cartesian coordinates

annotations.note: The general inner-product formula is in `stereoToFun_apply`, but the explicit (x,y,z)→(X,Y) Cartesian coordinate formulas are not separately spelled out.

annotations.label: Spherical-coordinate formulas

anchor.snippet: In spherical coordinates

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spherical-coordinate expression for stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Cylindrical-coordinate formulas

anchor.snippet: In cylindrical coordinates

annotations.note: Mathlib has no cylindrical-coordinate expression for stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Projection onto tangent plane at south pole

anchor.snippet: define stereographic projection from the north pole

annotations.note: Mathlib's `stereographic` projects from `v` onto the equatorial hyperplane `(ℝ∙v)ᗮ`, not the tangent plane at `−v`, so the convention differs by a factor of 2.

annotations.label: Equator maps to circle of radius 2

anchor.snippet: this projection sends the equator to the circle of radius 2 centered at the origin

annotations.note: The image of the equator under the equatorial-plane stereographic projection is not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Half-radius sphere convention

anchor.snippet: Other authors

annotations.note: Mathlib only formalizes the unit sphere convention; no half-radius variant is defined.

annotations.label: General stereographic projection from a point

anchor.snippet: In general, one can define a stereographic projection from any point

annotations.note: `stereographic hv` is parametrized by an arbitrary unit vector `v`, exactly capturing projection from a chosen point on the sphere.

annotations.label: Stereographic projection on n-sphere

anchor.snippet: stereographic projection may be applied to the unit

annotations.note: `stereographic'` packages stereographic projection on the unit sphere of any `(n+1)`-dimensional real inner product space as an open partial homeomorphism with target `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)`.

annotations.label: Stereographic projection on quadric hypersurface

anchor.snippet: Still more generally, suppose that

annotations.note: Mathlib's stereographic construction is restricted to spheres; no version for general quadric hypersurfaces exists.

annotations.label: Quadric as rational hypersurface

anchor.snippet: The stereographic projection presents the quadric hypersurface as a rational hypersurface

annotations.note: No statement about rationality of quadric hypersurfaces via stereographic projection appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Image of south pole, equator, hemispheres

anchor.snippet: sends the "south pole"

annotations.note: `stereographic_apply_neg` proves the antipode of the projection point maps to 0, but Mathlib has no separate statements about the equator or hemispheres.

annotations.label: Behavior near projection point

anchor.snippet: The projection is not defined at the projection point

annotations.note: `stereographic_source` shows the source is `{⟨v,_⟩}ᶜ`, i.e., the projection is defined exactly on the sphere minus the projection point.

annotations.label: Sphere as one-point compactification

anchor.snippet: the sphere is homeomorphic to the one-point compactification of the plane

annotations.note: `onePointEquivSphereOfFinrankEq` (and `onePointHyperplaneHomeoUnitSphere`) build the homeomorphism between the one-point compactification of a hyperplane/vector space and the unit sphere.

annotations.label: Rationality of point and image

anchor.snippet: either both are rational points or none of them

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state preservation of rational points by stereographic projection on the sphere.

annotations.label: Conformality and non-area-preservation

anchor.snippet: Stereographic projection is conformal, meaning that it preserves the angles

annotations.note: Conformality of `stereographic` is not proved in Mathlib; `conformalGroupoid` exists abstractly but is not connected to the sphere.

annotations.label: Area element formula

anchor.snippet: The area element is given in

annotations.note: No area-element formula for stereographic projection is given in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scale factors near equator and origin

anchor.snippet: Along the unit circle

annotations.note: Mathlib does not compute scale factors of stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Metric formula on the plane

anchor.snippet: The metric is given in

annotations.note: The pullback Riemannian metric formula on the plane is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No conformal area-preserving map sphere→plane

anchor.snippet: No map from the sphere to the plane can be both conformal and area-preserving

annotations.note: This obstruction theorem (a consequence of nonzero Gaussian curvature) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circles map to circles or lines

anchor.snippet: Circles on the sphere that do not pass through the point of projection are projected to circles on the plane

annotations.note: The circle-preservation theorem for stereographic projection is not proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lines in plane lift to circles through projection point

anchor.snippet: All lines in the plane, when transformed to circles on the sphere by the inverse of stereographic projection, meet at the projection point

annotations.note: Mathlib has no statement about the preimage of straight lines under inverse stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Loxodromes map to logarithmic spirals

anchor.snippet: The loxodromes of the sphere map to curves on the plane

annotations.note: Loxodromes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relation to plane inversion

anchor.snippet: The stereographic projection relates to the plane inversion in a simple way

annotations.note: Although `inversion` exists in `Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.Inversion.Basic`, its relation to stereographic projection is explicitly flagged as a TODO in the Sphere instances file.

annotations.label: Antipodal projections are inversive images

anchor.snippet: then P ′ and P″ are inversive images of each other in the unit circle

annotations.note: Mathlib does not relate north-pole and south-pole stereographic projections via plane inversion.

annotations.label: Metric tensor of stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: The associated metric tensor of the specific stereographic projection

annotations.note: The Riemannian metric tensor pulled back via stereographic projection is not formalized.

annotations.label: Wulff net

anchor.snippet: This special graph paper is called a stereonet or Wulff net

annotations.note: The Wulff net/stereonet is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area distortion ratio approaches 4

anchor.snippet: If the grid is made finer, this ratio approaches exactly 4

annotations.note: No statement about the limiting area-distortion ratio of stereographic projection appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonality of parallels and meridians on Wulff net

anchor.snippet: On the Wulff net, the images of the parallels and meridians intersect at right angles

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks any treatment of the Wulff net's orthogonality.

annotations.label: Plotting a point on the Wulff net

anchor.snippet: For an example of the use of the Wulff net

annotations.note: No Wulff-net plotting procedure is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two-projection cover of the sphere

anchor.snippet: the entire sphere can be mapped using two projections from distinct projection points

annotations.note: The charted-space instance uses many stereographic charts (one per point) and does not single out a two-chart cover, although individual `stereographic'` charts have target `Set.univ`.

annotations.label: Stereographic projection in complex coordinates

anchor.snippet: The stereographic projection from the north pole onto the equatorial plane is then

annotations.note: Mathlib's `stereographic` specializes to the complex case (sphere in ℂ via `Circle`) but the projection is not separately written in terms of a complex coordinate ζ.

annotations.label: South-pole projection and transition maps

anchor.snippet: define a stereographic projection from the south pole onto the equatorial plane

annotations.note: Smoothness of the transition maps between any pair of `stereographic'` charts is the core of the `IsManifold` instance, generalizing the north/south transition `ζ ↦ 1/ζ̄`.

annotations.label: Standard metric agrees with Fubini–Study

anchor.snippet: The standard metric on the unit sphere agrees with the Fubini–Study metric on the Riemann sphere

annotations.note: The Fubini–Study metric is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Disk visualization of real projective plane

anchor.snippet: one can visualize it as a disk

annotations.note: Mathlib has no disk-model visualization of ℝP².

annotations.label: Planes through origin map to circles

anchor.snippet: every plane through the origin intersects the unit sphere in a great circle

annotations.note: The image of great circles under stereographic projection is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pole of a plane

anchor.snippet: Further associated with each plane is a unique line, called the plane's pole

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the pole of a plane in this projective-visualization sense.

annotations.label: Schlegel diagram

anchor.snippet: In a Schlegel diagram

annotations.note: Schlegel diagrams of polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pythagorean triples via stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: stereographic projection from the unit circle provides a means to describe all primitive Pythagorean triples

annotations.note: `circleEquivGen` parametrizes the unit circle via `t ↦ (2t/(1+t²), (1−t²)/(1+t²))` and is used to classify Pythagorean triples.

annotations.label: Bijection of rationals on circle and x-axis

anchor.snippet: gives a one-to-one correspondence between the rational number points

annotations.note: `circleEquivGen` is an explicit `Equiv` between the parameter field `K` and circle points with `y ≠ −1`; specializing to `K = ℚ` yields the rational-points correspondence.

annotations.label: Tangent half-angle parametrization

anchor.snippet: The stereographic projection gives an alternative parametrization of the unit circle

annotations.note: The formulas `sin x = 2t/(1+t²)`, `cos x = (1−t²)/(1+t²)` with `t = tan(x/2)` are stated explicitly (also `cos_eq_…` and `tan_eq_…`), and `circleEquivGen` packages the same parametrization as an equivalence.

annotations.label: Length element under reparametrization

anchor.snippet: the length element

annotations.note: The length element `dx = 2 dt/(1+t²)` of the tangent half-angle substitution is not stated as a lemma in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No accurate angle+area map

anchor.snippet: no map from the sphere to the plane can accurately represent both angles and areas

annotations.note: This cartographic impossibility (Gauss-Bonnet/Theorema Egregium consequence) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polar aspect: meridians to rays, parallels to circles

anchor.snippet: It sends meridians to rays emanating from the origin and parallels to circles centered at the origin

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the image of meridians/parallels under polar stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Unique all-circles-to-circles projection

anchor.snippet: The stereographic is the only projection that maps all circles on a sphere to circles on a plane

annotations.note: This uniqueness characterization of stereographic projection is not formalized in Mathlib.
